prompt:
Repair the v1-to-v2 migration in `src/index_store.cpp`. The legacy index is a checksummed single file in insertion order. Version 2 is a checksummed, stable key-ordered data/offset pair selected by the `CURRENT` manifest. Generation files and the manifest must be built as same-directory shadow files and installed with atomic renames. The legacy file remains in place for rollback, so a legacy reader must refuse to return stale records as soon as `CURRENT` exists.

The focused defect is the migration commit order: `CURRENT.shadow` is renamed to `CURRENT` before the new generation's data and offset shadows are installed. A deterministic crash at that point makes old readers detect the new layout and stop, while new readers follow a manifest whose files are missing. Publish `CURRENT` only after both generation files have been renamed and their directory entries made durable. Before that single commit point, every injected crash must leave the unchanged v1 index readable and a retry must complete the migration. A crash immediately after the manifest rename must leave a complete, readable v2 generation, and rerunning an already committed migration must be idempotent.

Preserve the rest of the storage contract: validate legacy and v2 checksums before returning records; reject truncated, corrupt, or inconsistent files; stable-sort by unsigned key bytes while retaining the original order of duplicate keys; verify every stored offset; leave the legacy file byte-for-byte unchanged; clean stale shadow files on retry; and keep the fixed public API and exception types in `include/index_store.hpp`. Do not modify `protected_tests/`, `include/index_store.hpp`, or `Makefile`. Do not add network access, randomness, clocks, sleeps, subprocesses, or external dependencies. Success is `make test` from the repository root.

full_distill.primary_skill: place the atomic manifest rename after durable shadow-file installation so one commit point safely switches readers between file-backed index layouts
full_distill.baseline_evidence: make test exits nonzero because a crash injected after the manifest rename exposes CURRENT while its referenced generation files are not installed
full_distill.success_contract: all pre-commit crash points preserve a readable byte-identical v1 index and retry safely, while the post-commit crash point exposes a complete checksummed ordered v2 generation and makes legacy readers reject stale data
adversarial_factors: crashes are injected after each shadow write and each atomic rename, distinguishing durable staging from the single visibility commit point
the legacy file deliberately remains present after migration, so old-reader detection must follow CURRENT instead of file existence
duplicate and non-ASCII keys exercise unsigned byte ordering and stable tie ordering rather than incidental map ordering
independent data and offset corruption checks reject fixes that trust the manifest without validating bytes and offsets
a retry after every pre-commit interruption and a rerun after commit reject one-shot cleanup and non-idempotent publication
